zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Walwyn於1337年出生在英國英格蘭賀福爾姆 馬可爾時，他的父親Sir Thomas Walwyn27歲，母親Catherine Greyndour22歲。他於1363年與Joan De Helyon在英國英格蘭賀福爾海倫斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1393年之前在英國英格蘭賀福爾海倫斯，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Walwyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Wal(d)wine, which is of ancient Germanic origin, from elements meaning ‘power’ + ‘friend’.

from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Walweyn, Walwain. The central French form of this name is Gawain (see Gavin ). This name is found in the medieval Arthurian romances and is traditionally explained as an adaptation of the Welsh name Gwalchmei, perhaps meaning ‘hawk of the plain’.

variants.name: Gavin
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Gábháin or Ó Gáibhín, both of which are derived from diminutives of gábhadh ‘want’ or ‘danger’ (the second being the more likely meaning here).

Scottish and northern English: from the personal name Gavin, Scottish form of English Gawain and French Gauvain . The name was introduced into English from French versions of the Arthurian romances, where this name was borne by one of the knights of the Round Table, brother of Galahad and Mordred and a nephew of Arthur. It has been taken to be an adaptation of the Welsh name Gwalchmei, perhaps ‘hawk of the plain’.
Scottish: shortened form of McGavin , which is believed to be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gobhann ‘son of the smith’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Walwyn was born in 1337, in Much Marcle, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Thomas Walwyn, was 27 and his mother, Catherine Greyndour, was 22. He married Joan De Helyon in 1363, in Hellens, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died before 1393, in Hellens, Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.St Bartholomew's Church, Much Marcle, He: 영국 허퍼드실르 무치 마클 스트 바토롬w'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Walwyn(이)가 1337년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 무치 마클에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Walwyn(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine Greyndour(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1363년 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 헬렌스에서 Joan De Helyon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1393년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르 헬렌스에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

ru.St Bartholomew's Church, Much Marcle, He: ст бартоломю'с чурч, муч маркл, хирефордшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Walwyn родился в 1337 г. в местности муч маркл, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Walwyn было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine Greyndour, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan De Helyon в 1363 г. в местности хелленс, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер перед 1393 г. в местности хелленс, хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Walwyn nació en 1337, en Much Marcle, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Thomas Walwyn, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Catherine Greyndour, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Joan De Helyon en 1363, en Hellens, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió antes de 1393, en Hellens, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
